epigraph.text: For everything there is a season, and a time for every matter under heaven.
epigraph.quote: Time is what keeps everything from happening at once.

content.text:
The exponential distribution models waiting times—the time until the 
next event in a Poisson process. The gamma distribution generalizes 
this to the time until multiple events.

content.title: Exponential Distribution
content.content:
A random variable $X$ has the **exponential distribution** 
with rate $\lambda > 0$, written $X \sim \Exp(\lambda)$, if:
\[
 f(x) = \lambda e^{-\lambda x}, \quad x \geq 0
\]

content.title: Exponential Probability
content.problem:
Customers arrive at a store at rate 4 per hour (Poisson process). 
What is the probability that the time between arrivals exceeds 30 minutes?
content.solution:
Let $X$ = time until next arrival (in hours). $X \sim \Exp(4)$.
\[
 P(X > 0.5) = \int_{0.5}^{\infty} 4e^{-4x} \, dx = e^{-4(0.5)} = e^{-2} \approx 0.135
\]

content.title: Memoryless Property of Exponential
content.content:
If $X \sim \Exp(\lambda)$, then for all $s, t > 0$:
\[
 P(X > s + t \mid X > s) = P(X > t)
\]
The exponential is the *only* continuous memoryless distribution.
content.label: Theorem

content.text:
If you've been waiting 10 minutes for a bus, the remaining wait time 
has the same distribution as if you'd just arrived.

content.title: Gamma Distribution
content.content:
$X$ has a **gamma distribution** with 
shape $\alpha > 0$ and rate $\lambda > 0$, written $X \sim \Gamma(\alpha, \lambda)$, if:
\[
 f(x) = \frac{\lambda^\alpha}{\Gamma(\alpha)} x^{\alpha-1} e^{-\lambda x}, \quad x > 0
\]
where $\Gamma(\alpha) = \int_0^\infty t^{\alpha-1} e^{-t} \, dt$ is the 
gamma function.

content.text:
The exponential is $\Gamma(1, \lambda)$. The sum of $n$ independent 
$\Exp(\lambda)$ variables is $\Gamma(n, \lambda)$.

marginNotes.text: My times are in your hand.
